Details:
Removal of mercury by granular activated carbon (50 to greater than 99 percent removal) was documented in sorption and bench-scale studies. In one study evaluating the effectiveness of different GAC media types, Westvaco's Nuchar SA appeared to perform the best over the widest range in pH (2.5 to 11). Nuchar SA also seemed to perform well with varying concentrations and competing ions, all of which affect mercury removal. In a few limited tests, organic mercury compounds were found to adsorb onto GAC better than inorganic mercury compounds.
In three EPA Environmental Technology Verification studies, removal of inorganic mercury by GAC in POU or POE units ranged 95 to 99 percent.  
GAC is a Best Available Technology for mercury control.
